channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Airfares from Australia to Europe rise due to Middle East conflict and fuel costs
item.description: Airfares from Australia to Europe are expected to rise significantly as the ongoing conflict in the Middle East disrupts aviation operations and fuels spike in price. Qantas has warned of potential ti...

item.title: How Leading Fund Managers Navigate Volatile Global Markets
item.description: The global investment landscape has become increasingly complex due to geopolitical tensions, rapid technological change, and shifting economic alliances. In this context, long-term investors are seek...

item.title: Kim Kardashian and Kris Jenner Deny Involvement in Sex Tape Release Amid Legal Dispute
item.description: Kim Kardashian has denied under oath that she or her mother, Kris Jenner, planned the release of her 2007 sex tape with Ray J. These sworn statements, filed on March 11, 2026, are part of an ongoing d...

item.title: NBI seizes P12 billion in counterfeit US dollars in Pasay, arrests suspect
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) successfully confiscated an estimated P12 billion worth of counterfeit US dollars in a major operation in Pasay City. Authorities also apprehended the main s...

item.title: FBI investigates Michigan synagogue attack by Lebanese-born U.S. citizen following family deaths in Lebanon airstrike
item.description: Federal investigators have raided the home of Ayman Mohamad Ghazali, a 41-year-old Lebanese-born U.S. citizen accused of launching a violent attack against Temple Israel in West Bloomfield Township, M...

item.title: Australian Government's Response to US Military Requests Remains Unclear
item.description: Almost two weeks into the US-Israeli-led conflict with Iran, key details about Australia's involvement remain undisclosed. Prime Minister Anthony Albanese recently announced the deployment of an E-7A ...

item.title: Study highlights family concerns over Canadian MAID process and communication gaps
item.description: A recent study by the University of Alberta explored the experiences of Canadian families whose loved ones received Medical Assistance in Dying (MAID). While Canada’s assisted-death law centres on ind...

item.title: Sophie Oluwole: Championing Yoruba Thought and African Philosophy
item.description: Sophie Oluwole (1935-2018) was a trailblazing Nigerian academic, the first woman in her country to earn a PhD in philosophy. She brought the rich Yoruba intellectual tradition into global academic con...

item.title: Debate on HALEU-Th Fuel Viability for India’s Nuclear Reactors
item.description: A recent study published in the journal Current Science by scientists from Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC) has triggered strong debate within the Indian nuclear community. The report questioned t...

item.title: Health Risks Linked to Chronic Sleep Deprivation
item.description: As the world celebrate World Sleep Day on March 13, 2026, health experts in Tanzania and worldwide remind people say proper sleep is vital for both mind and body wellbeing. Specialists compare sleep w...

item.title: Sokoto Government Approves Early March Salary Payment Ahead of Eid
item.description: Sokoto State government don approve say dem go pay workers their March salary early, ahead of the Eid-el-Fitr celebration. Governor Ahmed Aliyu give green light make payment start from Friday, March 1...

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Criticizes Trump Over Gas Prices and Iran Comments
item.description: On his Thursday night show, late-night host Jimmy Kimmel criticized President Donald Trump for his recent comments on rising gas prices amid the ongoing war between Israel and Iran. Kimmel said Trump ...

item.title: Seven police officers killed in IED attack on patrol vehicle in Lakki Marwat, KP
item.description: On Friday, a tragic incident occurred in the Bettani subdivision of Lakki Marwat, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, where seven policemen, including the Station House Officer (SHO), were killed in a terrorist attac...

item.title: Man Killed After Driving Truck Into Temple Israel Synagogue in Michigan
item.description: On Thursday, a 41-year-old man lost his life after driving his pickup truck into the Temple Israel synagogue on the outskirts of Detroit, Michigan, setting off a fire and prompting a large police resp...

item.title: George Russell secures Mercedes’ top spot for China Grand Prix sprint
item.description: George Russell has delivered an impressive performance at the Shanghai International Circuit, claiming pole position for the first sprint race of the 2026 Formula One season in China. Leading from sta...

item.title: Urgent repairs and safety measures needed at deadly bridge blackspot
item.description: This editorial focuses on a notorious bridge in Kenya that has gained a grim reputation as a ‘killer’ crossing due to frequent accidents and repeated loss of life. It argues that Kenyans should not ha...

item.title: Severe Windstorm Topples Semi-Trucks and Closes Part of Coquihalla Highway in B.C.
item.description: A powerful windstorm swept through British Columbia’s Fraser Valley and Hope area on Wednesday night, producing winds equivalent to a Category 1 hurricane. The extreme gusts, which peaked at 139 km/h ...

item.title: CBK Reports Stable Kenyan Shilling Backed by KSh 1.9 Trillion Forex Reserves
item.description: According to the Central Bank of Kenya’s latest data, the Kenyan shilling has maintained stability against the US dollar and other East African Community (EAC) currencies. As of Friday, March 13, 2026...

item.title: Ombudsman to file charges against eight House members over flood control fund irregularities
item.description: The Independent Commission for Infrastructure (ICI) has recommended filing criminal charges against eight members of the House of Representatives regarding anomalies in flood control projects, Ombudsm...

item.title: March Break Activities and Events in Ottawa
item.description: The article provides a comprehensive overview of activities and events available in Ottawa over March Break, offering options for families, students, and tourists. It highlights local parks reopening ...
